title: Roman Anthony: Red Sox 2-7 start is ‘unacceptable to the fans’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Red Sox have started the season 2-7, marking their worst start since 2019.
Roman Anthony stated the team's poor performance is 'unacceptable' to both fans and their own standards.
Boston ranks defensively in the bottom of the league with nine errors, tied for third-most.

title: Is this really Boston’s most annoying escalator?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: A Reddit post claims the up escalator from Prudential skybridge to Copley Place is often out of service.
Commenters suggested other contenders for Boston's most annoying escalator, including those at Porter Square and Logan Airport.
Boston.com invites readers to share their opinions on the city's most frustrating escalators via a provided form or email.

title: NFL analyst predicts two possible Patriots draft trades
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots hold the 31st pick and have 11 selections in the 2026 NFL Draft.
ESPN analyst Bill Barnwell predicts a potential trade with the Browns, moving to the 24th pick.
Barnwell also suggests a trade down scenario with the Jets, acquiring the 33rd and 44th picks.

title: Painter claiming injury at Bill Belichick’s house sues him over alleged unsafe work site
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Painter Andrew Jackson sues Bill Belichick for $285,436, alleging unsafe work conditions at Belichick's Nantucket home.
Jackson claims he suffered a severe ankle injury due to unsafe plastic sheeting at 45 Fair Street on June 21, 2024.
Belichick purchased the 45 Fair Street property in 2024 for $4.8 million and owns several other Nantucket properties.

title: Woman shot and killed by police in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A woman was fatally shot by police responding to a domestic disturbance in Northfield, New Hampshire, early Monday.
The identities of the deceased woman and the officer involved have not been released. An autopsy is scheduled.
This incident marks the third use of deadly force by police in New England since Saturday.

title: Man accused of assaulting two workers, stealing car during 3 separate trips to Dedham gas station
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: John Laurent, 35, arrested for assault and car theft at Dedham gas station on Saturday.
Police responded to Sam's Express for two assaults and a stolen vehicle in separate incidents.
Laurent faces charges of assault, trespassing, and larceny; arraignment set for Monday in Dedham.

title: Mass. business confidence dips back into pessimism
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts business confidence index fell to 47 in March due to energy prices and geopolitical tensions.
The U.S. added 178,000 jobs last month, while unemployment remained stable at 4.3%.
Rising energy costs and trade policies are increasing caution among Massachusetts employers, impacting confidence.

title: For $6.5m, this Connecticut inn comes with a liquor license in trendy Mystic
author: Regina Cole
bullets: The Captain's Mansion, a boutique hotel in Mystic, Conn., is listed for $6.5 million as a turnkey property.
The 10,335-square-foot Victorian mansion includes eight bedrooms, a commercial kitchen, and several restored historic features.
The sale includes a liquor license, furniture, fixtures, and zoning for both business and residential use.

title: Fishing crew rescued near Gloucester after boat runs aground amid harsh seas
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two fishing crew members were rescued near Gloucester after their 40-foot boat ran aground on Saturday night.
The Gloucester Fire and Police Departments, along with the Harbormaster, coordinated the rescue on 252 Atlantic Road.
Despite 2 to 4-foot waves causing damage, the U.S. Coast Guard assisted but couldn't free the boat; the crew was unharmed.

title: The Rafael Devers trade is already haunting the Red Sox in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox received four players from the Giants in the Rafael Devers trade, but only one remains with Boston.
Boston's 2026 season starts poorly at 2-7, missing the impact of Devers' bat and struggling defensively with nine errors.
Devers' replacement, Ranger Suarez, signed for $130 million, is 0-1 with an 8.64 ERA in his first two starts.
